reference: My son is eleven years old. He struggles at school and was diagnosed with Attention Deficit Disorder. He is taking medication for Attention Deficit Disorder. We recently took him to a psychologist because he became very depressed and could not get along with the teacher. She administered an intelligence quotient test and found that he has an above-average intelligence quotient. She suspects Asperger's syndrome. We also took him for a blood test, and we received the results that his thyroid does not function properly. Could this possibly cause some of his problems?
text: My son is 11 years old, he struggles at school and was diagnosed with attention deficit disorder. He is taking medication for attention deficit disorder. We recently took him to a psychologist because he became very depressed and could not get along with the teacher. She administered an intelligence quotient test and found that he has an above-average intelligence quotient; she suspects Asperger's syndrome. We also took him for a blood test, and we received the results that his thyroid does not function properly. Could this possibly cause some of his problems?
